title: githopsis diffusa subsp. guadalupensis
intro: Githopsis diffusa subsp. guadalupensis, also known as Guadalupe Purplecups, is a perennial plant species in the bellflower family that is native to the Guadalupe Mountains in Texas and New Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Githopsis diffusa subsp. guadalupensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, although further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of its use as a medicinal herb.
